message:
chore(core): rustfmt module ordering and slice predicate

Two small style cleanups in frankenlibc-core that bring the source in
line with rustfmt's expected output.

src/lib.rs:
- Reorder the `proc_maps` mod declaration above `process` so the public
  module list is alphabetical, matching the surrounding sort order.

src/proc_maps/mod.rs:
- Replace the closure form `trim_end_matches(|c: char| c == '\n' || c
  == '\r')` with the slice form `trim_end_matches(['\n', '\r'])`. Same
  semantics; the slice form is the idiomatic shape and is what rustfmt
  / clippy prefer.
- Collapse the wrapped `parse_maps_line(...)` test call back to a
  single line so it matches rustfmt's max-width formatting now that
  the call signature comfortably fits.

No behavior changes.
